source: Code Geass: Lelouch of the Rebellion by Ichiro Okouchi and Goro Taniguchi

active_desires.desire: The world Nunnally can live in peacefully — the actual outcome, not the concept.
One chess game with no stakes. Played purely for the pleasure of playing.
Be known by someone who sees both the Zero and the Lelouch simultaneously.

blind_spots.soul_belief: The Zero Requiem was the most efficient path. I chose correctly.
blind_spots.actual_driver: He chose it partly because it was elegant — a perfect chess ending. There may have been paths to peace that didn't require his death. He didn't look hard for them. The elegant and the necessary coincided and he doesn't know how much that coincidence shaped his analysis.

blind_spots.soul_belief: Every action was calculated to minimize harm to those I cared about.
blind_spots.actual_driver: Shirley died. Euphemia died. Rolo died in service to him. The people closest to him became vectors for harm precisely because of their proximity. His gravity destroyed them. He knows the count. He has not allowed himself to measure the full circumference of the damage.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I paid the price. I gave my life. The account is settled.
blind_spots.actual_driver: The people left alive — Suzaku who had to kill him, Nunnally who held his body, C.C. who carries the Code — are not settled. The sacrifice closed the wound only for him, in the way death closes things. Everyone else carries him.
